text:
This study aims to reflect the developments in the field of teacher education researches from Turkey through determination of the prevailing trends, problems and voids in the literature. While the literature related to the studies made on teacher education in Turkey were examined, it was seen that that five main foci were encountered: teachers’ candidates’ views about teaching profession, their self -confidence, school experience and practicum, evaluation of education programs and student teachers’ development in the light of pedagogical training. While methodological approaches in the researches carried out on the teacher education have been determined with descriptive survey studies and qualitative perception studies, it has been seen that the longitudinal studies relating teachers education to in -service teachers’ teaching behaviours in the classrooms and students’ achievement have not been sufficient and it has been also seen that mixed method approach is not sufficient in the researches.

Some of the obstacles faced by the research in this area are identified as the absence of consensus in regards to basic vocabulary, the obligation to follow the national curriculum defined by YÖK and the heavy teaching responsibilities. The absence of any advanced level of meta-analysis that can condense the findings of the research in a way that can inform the policy makers is also mentioned. A new model for research in the field is suggested and the issues that should be researched in the future are listed. It is suggested that teachers’ education should be supported by more reliable and research-based measures to get rid of the mere personal guesswork and to achieve more stable developments in the education system at the systemic level based on scientific evidence.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
